__comment: The Park Bulletin (/now). One page per NPS Yosemite Guide edition: when the park publishes a new Guide (roughly every five weeks), rewrite this file from it, then (1) bump BULLETIN_URL's ?v= in page-now.jsx and HOME_BULLETIN_URL in page-home.jsx, (2) run `npm --prefix scripts run compile` and `npm --prefix scripts run seo`, (3) commit. edition.updated drives the /now sitemap lastmod, and edge/seo.js imports this file to serve the bulletin to non-JS crawlers. Facts come from the printed Guide plus the journal's own observation; do not carry a stale edition past its end date without a note. Event entries carry an `end` ISO date so the page can dim what has passed mid-edition.

edition.label: July 15 – August 18, 2026
edition.lede: The midsummer edition. Every road in the park is open, the waterfalls are thinning, the Mist Trail is under weekday repairs, and the Obata centennial weekend opens the run. Evenings in the high country are the move.
edition.source: Condensed from the National Park Service Yosemite Guide for July 15 – August 18, 2026, with the journal's own notes.

alerts: Mist Trail closed for trail work Monday through Thursday, 7 am to 3:30 pm. Hike the Vernal and Nevada corridor Friday through Sunday, or start after the crews knock off.
Yosemite Falls is thin and fading for the season, and Mirror Lake is dry. Bridalveil is still worth the stop.
Wawona Hotel and its dining room are closed for renovation. White Wolf lodge, store, and dining room are closed this season.
Raft rentals on the Merced are done for the year.
EV charging in Tuolumne Meadows is temporarily unavailable. Charge in the Valley, El Portal, or Wawona.
Valley parking fills by late morning. Arrive before 8 am or after 4 pm, and text ynptraffic to 333111 for traffic alerts.
High Sierra Camps at Glen Aulin, Sunrise, and May Lake are open.

areas.name: Yosemite Valley
areas.note: Busy by mid-morning, worse on weekends. Parking in three hubs: Curry Village, Yosemite Village, Yosemite Falls. Once parked, stay parked and ride the free shuttles.

areas.name: Tioga Road & Tuolumne Meadows
areas.note: The 39-mile high-country drive, 6,200 to nearly 10,000 feet. Little to no cell service; fuel and plan before you go.

areas.name: Glacier Point Road
areas.note: One hour from the Valley. No trailers or vehicles over 30 feet past the Sentinel Dome trailhead.

areas.name: Wawona & Mariposa Grove
areas.note: Free grove shuttle every 15 minutes, 8 am to 7 pm, last bus down at 8 pm. The hotel is closed for renovation.

areas.name: Hetch Hetchy
areas.note: No services, very little shade, and hot. No vehicles over 25 feet on the entrance road.

areas.name: Gas
areas.note: El Portal, Crane Flat, and Wawona stations. All pump 24 hours with a card.

valleyDay.title: Junior Ranger Discovery Table, to 3 pm
Wee Wild Ones, kids 10 and under

valleyDayNote: Everything unmarked is free and drop-in. Also running: the Valley Floor Tour by tram (10 am, 11 am, 1 pm, 2 pm, and sunset, ticketed), free photography walks from The Ansel Adams Gallery (Tu and Th 9 am, registration required), and the kids' open art studio at Happy Isles (10 am – 3 pm daily).

items: Bird walk W and Sa 7:30 am from Lembert Dome parking; climber coffee Su 9 am at the store.
A walk leaves at 10 am every day: wildflowers Su Tu, domes and meadows M Th, geology W Sa, Tioga Pass botany F, junior rangers W Sa.
Parsons Memorial Lodge open daily 10 am – 4 pm at the Soda Springs.
Afternoon walks most days at 1 or 2 pm: forest M, Dog Lake hike Tu, the river W, wildlife Th, history F.
Sunset on the Dome F 6 pm (not Jul 24). Campfire program nightly 8 pm at the campground (not Aug 15). Stars Over Tuolumne M W Sa 9:30 pm (not Aug 15).
Coffee with a ranger W and Su 9 am at Pine Tree Market.
Nature walk daily 9:30 am from the Mariposa Grove arrival area; junior ranger talk daily 1:30 pm in the grove.
Giant sequoia hike M W Th 11 am in the Tuolumne Grove; junior ranger table daily 2 – 3:30 pm at the trailhead.
Evening program Su W F Sa 7 pm at the Crane Flat campground amphitheater; Stars Over Crane Flat 8:30 pm on select nights, register at Big Oak Flat.
Glacier Point Starry Skies 9 pm Su – Th, ticketed (dark Jul 26 – 30). Free astronomy-club star parties on select Friday and Saturday nights: Jul 17 – 18, Jul 31 – Aug 1, Aug 7 – 8, Aug 14 – 15.

events.title: Chiura Obata exhibit
events.where: The Ansel Adams Gallery, 9 am – 5 pm
events.note: Original Obata pieces from the park's collection.

events.title: Obata Art Weekend
events.note: Workshops, demos, and ranger talks for the centennial of Obata's 1927 high-country summer. Register at Yosemite.org.

events.title: Obata's Yosemite, special screening
events.note: 15-minute film on the half hour, 9:30 am – 4 pm.

events.title: Parsons Memorial Lodge Summer Series
events.note: Talks, readings, and music at the Soda Springs. Free; schedule at the visitor center.

events.title: Geology Hut: Reading Yosemite's Rocks
events.note: Conservancy stewardship series. Register at Yosemite.org.

events.title: Parks to Celebrate the Country's 250th
events.note: America the Beautiful: photographs from the national parks.

events.title: Cultural demonstrations: basketry and beading
events.note: Charlene Redner and Rena Brown, Bishop Paiute.

events.title: Tom Killion: Trails and Trees
events.note: Woodblocks and prints, Aug 8 – Sep 26. Artist reception Aug 8, 1 – 3 pm.

events.title: Poetry workshops at Parsons Lodge
events.note: Michael Metivier (Aug 15) and Heather Swan (Aug 16). Bring pen and paper.

events.title: Yosemite Knapping Event
events.note: The art of making stone tools.

events.title: Cultural demonstrations: basketry
events.note: Sandy Clark, North Fork Rancheria of Mono Indians.

events.title: Yosemite Zero Heroes litter cleanup
events.note: Drop-in volunteer event, kids 5 and up welcome.

eventsNote: Conservancy art classes run all edition at Happy Isles (weekdays 9 am, register at Yosemite.org/art): printmaking through Jul 17, zine journaling Jul 20 – 24, nature drawing Jul 27 – Aug 7, watercolor Aug 10 – 14 and Aug 17 – 21.

trails.name: Mist Trail to Vernal & Nevada Falls
trails.note: Closed M – Th, 7 am – 3:30 pm for repairs. Footbridge 1.6 mi round trip, top of Vernal 2.4 mi, top of Nevada 5 mi. From Happy Isles, shuttle stop 16.

trails.name: Half Dome
trails.note: Cables are up. Daily lottery two days ahead at go.nps.gov/HDpermits.

trails.name: Lower Yosemite Fall
trails.note: 1 mi loop, shuttle stop 6. The falls typically go dry by late summer; go for the amphitheater, not the water.

trails.name: Mirror Lake
trails.note: 2 mi round trip on the paved road, shuttle stop 17. The lakebed is a meadow this time of year.

trails.name: Bridalveil Fall
trails.note: 0.5 mi from the parking area. Still showing in the afternoon wind.

trails.name: Cook's Meadow
trails.note: 1 mi flat loop, shuttle stop 6. Half Dome and Yosemite Falls views; best light early and late.

trails.name: Yosemite Falls Trail
trails.note: Columbia Rock, 2 mi round trip, earns the view. Full climb 7.2 mi, 2,700 ft, start at dawn. From Camp 4.

trails.name: Four Mile Trail
trails.note: 9.6 mi round trip to Glacier Point. One-way option: ride the tour bus up, walk down. Drop-offs only at the point.

trails.name: Valley Loop Trail
trails.note: Up to 11.5 mi, mostly flat, and the quietest miles on the floor.

trailsNote: Overnights require a wilderness permit year-round, and a bear canister. Carry a quart of water per person for every two hours; three for the falls corridor.

items.name: Wilderness centers (Valley, Tuolumne, Big Oak Flat, Wawona)

transit.name: Valley shuttles
transit.note: Free, daily 7 am – 10 pm. Valleywide green line hits every stop, every 22 – 32 minutes; the purple East Valley line runs every 18 – 22 minutes.

transit.name: Mariposa Grove shuttle
transit.note: Free, every 15 minutes, 8 am – 7 pm; last bus leaves the grove at 8 pm.

transit.name: Tuolumne Meadows shuttle
transit.note: Free, between the Meadows and Olmsted Point, 8 am – 4 pm.

transit.name: Hiker buses
transit.note: Tuolumne hikers bus once daily and the Glacier Point tour bus twice daily, both paid, both bookable at TravelYosemite.com. Glacier Point drops hikers one-way; no pickup at the point.

transit.name: YARTS
transit.note: Public transit into the park: Merced year-round; Fresno, Sonora, and Mammoth Lakes in summer. YARTS.com.

transit.name: EV charging
transit.note: The Ahwahnee, Curry Village, Welcome Center, Yosemite Falls parking, the Lodge, El Portal, and Wawona. Tuolumne chargers are down.

essentials.title: Bears
essentials.text: Food storage is federal law: within arm's reach by day, out of the car and into a locker by night. To a bear, food means anything with a scent. Sightings: 209/372-0322.

essentials.title: Lightning
essentials.text: Afternoon thunderstorms are the season's weather. Off the domes and exposed rim when storms build; shelter in a building or vehicle.

essentials.title: Smoke
essentials.text: Fire season runs from here into October. Check air quality at fire.airnow.gov the morning you drive in, not the night before.

essentials.title: Wildlife
essentials.text: 150 feet from bears and coyotes, 25 feet from everything else. Feeding or approaching any animal is illegal.

essentials.title: Heat & water
essentials.text: The Valley floor is hot by late morning. Walk early, carry a quart per person per two hours, and move uphill by noon.

essentials.title: Parking
essentials.text: Designated spaces or paved turnouts only, pulled fully off the road. Vehicles on vegetation get cited and towed.
